subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Blood Types and Transfusions
description: A 60-second lesson on Blood Types and Transfusions.

hook.text: Before blood types were discovered, transfusions were a coin flip between saving and killing.

buildup.text: Karl Landsteiner discovered A, B, and O blood types in 1901, winning a Nobel Prize.

discovery.text: Mismatched blood triggers an immune attack—the body destroys the foreign red blood cells.

twist.text: Type O negative is the 'universal donor' but only 7% of people have it.

climax.text: Blood typing made safe transfusions possible and revolutionized trauma surgery.

punchline.text: Four letters—A, B, AB, O—made blood donation safe.

quiz.question: What happens when mismatched blood types are transfused?
options: The immune system attacks the foreign blood cells
